title: leymus mollis subsp. interior
intro: Leymus mollis subsp. interior, also known as the interior Leymus mollis, is a type of grass that belongs to the genus Leymus and is native to the interior regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The plant is also known to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic agent. The herbal preparations of Leymus mollis subsp. interior typically involve drying the plant material and then using it in teas, infusions, or topical applications to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
